 {"id":"bd-p2ip","title":"Implement review lock mechanism (flock-based)","description":"# Task: Implement Review Lock Mechanism

## Purpose
Prevent concurrent `ru review` runs which would cause chaos with overlapping worktrees, duplicate API calls, and conflicting state updates.

## Implementation Details

### Lock File Location
```bash
REVIEW_LOCK_FILE="$RU_STATE_DIR/review.lock"
```

### acquire_review_lock()
```bash
acquire_review_lock() {
    mkdir -p "$RU_STATE_DIR"
    
    # Open fd 200 for locking
    exec 200>"$REVIEW_LOCK_FILE"
    
    # Try non-blocking lock
    if ! flock -n 200; then
        # Check who holds it
        local holder_info=""
        if [[ -f "$REVIEW_LOCK_FILE.info" ]]; then
            holder_info=$(cat "$REVIEW_LOCK_FILE.info")
        fi
        log_error "Another review session is active"
        [[ -n "$holder_info" ]] && log_error "Started: $holder_info"
        log_info "Use 'ru review --status' to check, or wait for completion"
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Write lock info
    cat > "$REVIEW_LOCK_FILE.info" << EOF
{
  "run_id": "$REVIEW_RUN_ID",
  "started_at": "$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)",
  "pid": $$,
  "mode": "$mode"
}
EOF
    
    return 0
}
```

### release_review_lock()
```bash
release_review_lock() {
    # Remove info file
    rm -f "$REVIEW_LOCK_FILE.info"
    
    # Release flock
    flock -u 200 2>/dev/null || true
}
```

### Trap Handler Integration
```bash
# In cmd_review(), set up trap
trap 'release_review_lock; exit 130' INT TERM

# Ensure cleanup on any exit
trap_cleanup_review() {
    release_review_lock
    cleanup_stale_worktrees
}
trap trap_cleanup_review EXIT
```

### Stale Lock Detection
```bash
check_stale_lock() {
    if [[ -f "$REVIEW_LOCK_FILE.info" ]]; then
        local lock_pid
        lock_pid=$(jq -r '.pid' "$REVIEW_LOCK_FILE.info" 2>/dev/null)
        
        # Check if process still exists
        if [[ -n "$lock_pid" ]] && ! kill -0 "$lock_pid" 2>/dev/null; then
            log_warn "Found stale lock from dead process $lock_pid"
            rm -f "$REVIEW_LOCK_FILE.info"
            return 0  # Lock is stale
        fi
    fi
    return 1  # Lock is valid or doesn't exist
}
```

## Why flock?
- Atomic lock acquisition
- Automatically released on process exit (even crash)
- No race conditions
- Works on Linux, macOS, BSD
- Already used successfully in parallel sync

## Edge Cases
- Process killed with SIGKILL: Lock released by kernel, but .info file remains
- Machine reboot: Lock file and .info persist but are stale
- Multiple users: Each user has own state dir, so no conflict

## Testing
- Verify lock prevents second ru review
- Verify lock released on normal exit
- Verify lock released on Ctrl+C
- Verify stale lock detected after kill -9
- Verify lock info shows useful details

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Only one ru review can run at a time
- [ ] Lock info shows run_id, start time, PID
- [ ] Lock released on all exit paths
- [ ] Stale locks from crashed processes handled","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:16:53.209434378-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T16:56:47.476050107-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T16:56:47.476050107-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented full flock-based review lock mechanism with JSON info file (run_id, started_at, pid, mode). Added check_stale_lock() for dead process detection. Proper trap handlers for INT/TERM/EXIT. All acceptance criteria met: single concurrent review, detailed lock info, cleanup on all exit paths, stale lock detection.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-p2ip","depends_on_id":"bd-mnu9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:44:52.831071716-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-pfle","title":"Implement parallel preflight execution","description":"# Parallel Preflight Execution

## Parent Epic: bd-cpxq (Preflight Safety Checks)

## Purpose
Run all preflight checks upfront before starting any agents. Shows all problems at once for better UX.

## Implementation

```bash
run_parallel_preflight() {
    local -n repos_ref=$1
    local -a passed_repos=()
    local -a failed_repos=()
    local preflight_results_file="${AGENT_SWEEP_STATE_DIR}/preflight_results.ndjson"

    log_info "Running preflight checks on ${#repos_ref[@]} repositories..."

    # Initialize results file
    echo "{\"type\":\"header\",\"timestamp\":\"$(date -Iseconds)\"}" > "$preflight_results_file"

    # Run preflight for each repo (can be parallelized with xargs/parallel)
    for repo_spec in "${repos_ref[@]}"; do
        local repo_path
        repo_path=$(repo_spec_to_path "$repo_spec")

        if repo_preflight_check "$repo_path"; then
            passed_repos+=("$repo_spec")
            echo "{\"repo\":\"$repo_spec\",\"status\":\"passed\"}" >> "$preflight_results_file"
        else
            failed_repos+=("$repo_spec")
            echo "{\"repo\":\"$repo_spec\",\"status\":\"failed\",\"reason\":\"$PREFLIGHT_SKIP_REASON\"}" >> "$preflight_results_file"
            log_warn "Skipping $repo_spec: $PREFLIGHT_SKIP_REASON"
        fi
    done

    # Summary
    log_info "Preflight complete: ${#passed_repos[@]} passed, ${#failed_repos[@]} skipped"

    # Return passed repos
    repos_ref=("${passed_repos[@]}")

    # Return failure if all repos failed
    [[ ${#passed_repos[@]} -gt 0 ]]
}
```

## Integration
- Called from cmd_agent_sweep() after loading repos, before processing
- Replaces per-repo preflight in run_sequential/parallel_sweep
- Results stored for summary display

## Benefits
- Fail fast: shows all problems before starting any agents
- Better UX: user sees complete picture immediately
- Reduces wasted time: no agents spawned for repos that would fail preflight

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All repos checked before any agent starts
- [ ] Failed repos listed with reasons
- [ ] Passed repos returned for processing
- [ ] Preflight results saved for summary
- [ ] Works with --dry-run mode

 {"id":"bd-prgk","title":"Implement commit plan execution","description":"# Commit Plan Execution

## Parent Epic: bd-mkoc (Agent Sweep Command Implementation)

## Purpose
Execute validated commit plans using ru's deterministic git execution.

## Why ru Executes (Not Agent)
- Agent can ignore prompt constraints
- ru enforces security guardrails
- Deterministic, auditable execution
- Consistent with Planner→Validator→Executor model

## Implementation

```bash
# Execute commit plan after validation
# Args: $1=commit_plan_json, $2=repo_path
# Returns: 0=success, 1=failure
execute_commit_plan() {
    local plan="$1"
    local repo_path="$2"
    
    # Extract commits array
    local commits
    commits=$(json_get_field "$plan" "commits")
    
    # Parse each commit
    local commit_index=0
    while read -r commit_json; do
        ((commit_index++))
        
        local files message
        files=$(json_get_field "$commit_json" "files")
        message=$(json_get_field "$commit_json" "message")
        
        # Stage files
        for file in $files; do
            file="${file//\"/}"  # Strip quotes
            if [[ -f "$repo_path/$file" ]]; then
                git -C "$repo_path" add "$file" || {
                    log_error "Failed to stage: $file"
                    return 1
                }
            else
                log_warn "File not found, skipping: $file"
            fi
        done
        
        # Create commit
        if ! git -C "$repo_path" commit -m "$message" 2>&1; then
            log_error "Commit $commit_index failed"
            return 1
        fi
        
        log_verbose "Created commit $commit_index: ${message%%$'\n'*}"
    done <<< "$commits"
    
    # Push if requested
    local push_flag
    push_flag=$(json_get_field "$plan" "push")
    if [[ "$push_flag" == "true" ]]; then
        if ! git -C "$repo_path" push 2>&1; then
            log_error "Push failed"
            return 1
        fi
        log_verbose "Pushed to remote"
    fi
    
    return 0
}
```

## Commit Message Format
Agent should produce messages with:
- Subject line (50 chars max)
- Blank line
- Body explaining why
- Co-Authored-By trailer

Example:
```
feat(auth): implement OAuth2 PKCE flow

This commit adds PKCE support for mobile clients...

🤖 Generated with Claude Code
Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
```

## Execution Mode Check
```bash
if [[ "$AGENT_SWEEP_EXECUTION_MODE" == "plan" ]]; then
    log_info "Plan mode: Saving plan without execution"
    save_plan_to_artifacts "$plan" "$artifacts_dir/commit_plan.json"
    return 0
fi
```

## Post-Execution Validation
- Verify working tree is clean: git status --porcelain
- Verify commits created: git log --oneline -N
- If push=true, verify upstream: git status (should show "up to date")

## Error Recovery
- If commit fails: Do NOT continue with remaining commits
- Preserve partial state in artifacts
- Mark repo as failed with specific error","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-06T16:52:19.841468105-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-06T23:09:50.943637954-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-06T23:09:50.943637954-05:00","close_reason":"Completed","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-prgk","depends_on_id":"bd-65u3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-06T16:58:41.191291263-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-prgk","depends_on_id":"bd-nqjy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-06T16:58:41.21628008-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-prgk","depends_on_id":"bd-0ghe","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-06T16:58:41.235679564-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-prgk","depends_on_id":"bd-5iwb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-06T16:58:41.25636029-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-prgk","depends_on_id":"bd-8bxp","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-06T17:27:18.116549201-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
